memoryArtifacts.headline: Ross, Christina Livingston story  1345
memoryArtifacts.description:
When Christina Livingstone Ross was born in 1345, her father, William, was 35, and her mother, Christian, was 29. She married William Ross in Scotland. They had one child during their marriage.

Chris …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Graham於1295年出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾金加迪時，她的父親David de Graham Laird of Dundaff21歲，母親Isabella Erskine- of Perthshire24歲。她於1329年11月24日與Earl Hugh Ross of Ross在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1372年7月20日在英國蘇格蘭洛-爾費亞享年77歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭羅斯 安德 可洛馬迪希爾 本 費亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Graham于1295年出生在英国苏格兰培爾金加迪时，她的父亲David de Graham Laird of Dundaff21岁，母亲Isabella Erskine- of Perthshire24岁。她于1329年11月24日与Earl Hugh Ross of Ross在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1372年7月20日在英国苏格兰洛-爾費亞享年77岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰羅斯 安德 可洛馬迪希爾 本 費亞。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Graham was born in 1295, in Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, David de Graham Laird of Dundaff, was 21 and her mother, Isabella Erskine- of Perthshire, was 24. She married Earl Hugh Ross of Ross on 24 November 1329, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 20 July 1372, in Fearn, Ross-shire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 77, and was buried in Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotla: Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Écosse, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Graham est née en 1295, à Kincardine, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, David de Graham Laird of Dundaff, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Isabella Erskine- of Perthshire, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Earl Hugh Ross of Ross le 24 novembre 1329, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 juillet 1372, à Fearn, Ross-shire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat am 24. November 1329 in Scotland Earl Hugh Ross of Ross geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 20. Juli 1372 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Fearn, Ross-shire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Grahamが1295年にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアキンカーディンで生まれた時，父David de Graham Laird of Dundaffは21歳，母Isabella Erskine- of Perthshireは24歳であった。1329年11月24日にスコットランドでEarl Hugh Ross of Rossと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1372年7月20日にイギリススコットランドロス-シュアイアフエアルンで77歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドロス アンド クロマルトイヒル オフ フエアルンにて埋葬された。

ko.Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotla: 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티 힐 프 피른
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Graham(이)가 1295년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 킨카딘에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 David de Graham Laird of Dundaff(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Erskine- of Perthshire(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1329년 11월 24일 스컷랜드에서 Earl Hugh Ross of Ross(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1372년 7월 20일 영국 스코틀랜드 로스-실르 피른에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티 힐 프 피른에 묻혔다.

pt.Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotla: Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.Balnagown House, Easter Ross, Scotland,: Easter Ross, Highland, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Graham nasceu em 1295, em Kincardine, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, David de Graham Laird of Dundaff, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Erskine- of Perthshire, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Earl Hugh Ross of Ross em 24 de novembro de 1329, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de julho de 1372, em Fearn, Ross-shire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 77 anos, e foi sepultada em Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotla: хилл оф фирн, росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Graham родилась в 1295 г. в местности кинкардаин, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени David de Graham Laird of Dundaff было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Isabella Erskine- of Perthshire, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Earl Hugh Ross of Ross 24 ноября 1329 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 20 июля 1372 г. в местности фирн, росс-шир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности хилл оф фирн, росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Graham nació en 1295, en Kincardine, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, David de Graham Laird of Dundaff, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Isabella Erskine- of Perthshire, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Earl Hugh Ross of Ross el 24 de noviembre de 1329, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 20 de julio de 1372, en Fearn, Ross-shire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultada en Hill of Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Escocia, Reino Unido.
